sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The plotting.pyx module is a powerful and flexible component of the AnalysisG framework, designed for generating high-quality scientific plots. It leverages Cython for performance optimization and integrates seamlessly with popular Python plotting libraries such as matplotlib, mplhep (for ATLAS-style plots), boost_histogram for efficient histogramming, and numpy for numerical operations. This module provides base classes for common plotting tasks (1D and 2D histograms, line plots, ROC curves) and includes utilities for histogram manipulation and statistical tests.

sect2.title: ratio(H1, H2, axis, ylabel = "Ratio", normalize = False, yerror = False, this_hist = None)
sect2.para:
Calculates and plots the ratio of two histograms.
H1
The numerator histogram (boost_histogram object).
H2
The denominator histogram (boost_histogram object).
axis
The matplotlib axis object to plot on.
ylabel
The label for the y-axis of the ratio plot.
normalize
If True, normalizes both histograms before calculating the ratio.
yerror
If True, calculates and displays y-errors for the ratio.
this_hist
An instance of a plotting class (e.g., TH1F) to inherit font size and other styling.
A dictionary containing the ratio histogram, axis, color, histtype, markersize, and an axhline at 1.

sect2.title: chi2(H1, H2, axis, ylabel = "Ratio", normalize = False, yerror = False, this_hist = None)
sect2.para:
Calculates and plots a chi-squared-like difference between two histograms.
H1
The observed histogram (boost_histogram object).
H2
The expected histogram (boost_histogram object).
axis
The matplotlib axis object to plot on.
ylabel
The label for the y-axis (though it's overridden by a specific chi-squared expression).
normalize
If True, normalizes both histograms before calculating the difference.
yerror
If True, calculates and displays y-errors (currently set to 0).
this_hist
An instance of a plotting class (e.g., TH1F) to inherit font size and other styling.
A dictionary containing the difference histogram, axis, color, marker, histtype, markersize, and an additional string with the calculated chi-squared value.

sect1.title: The BasePlotting Class
sect1.para: BasePlotting is a cdef class that serves as the foundational class for all plotting objects within this module. It provides common functionalities such as figure initialization, plot resetting, saving figures, and managing various plot aesthetics and configurations.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent. Initializes a new C++ plotting object (self.ptr) and sets up matplotlib (self.matpl). Initializes flags for axis limits.
__init__(self, inpt = None): The Python initializer. If inpt (a dictionary) is provided, it attempts to set various attributes of self from inpt["data"].
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ plotting object pointed to by self.ptr.

cdef void __figure__(self, dict com = {"nrows" : 1, "ncols" : 1}): Creates a new matplotlib figure and axes based on the provided configuration com. It sets the figure size and attempts to set autoscale on the axes.

cdef void __resetplt__(self): Clears the current matplotlib figure, closes all figures, resets matplotlib's default runtime configuration parameters, and then calls __figure__ to create a new default figure.

cdef dict __compile__(self, bool raw = False): An abstract internal method intended to be overridden by subclasses. It's responsible for compiling the plot data and returning a dictionary of plotting parameters.
__reduce__(self): Implements the __reduce__ protocol for pickling BasePlotting objects, allowing them to be serialized and deserialized. It saves non-callable attributes.

dump(self, str path = "", str name = ""): Saves the current plotting object to a pickle file.
path
The directory path to save the file. Defaults to self.ptr.output_path.
name
The filename (without extension). Defaults to self.ptr.filename.

load(self, str path = "", str name = ""): Loads a plotting object from a pickle file.
path
The directory path to load from. Defaults to self.ptr.output_path.
name
The filename (without extension). Defaults to self.ptr.filename.
The loaded plotting object or None if loading fails.
__add__(self, BasePlotting other): Implements the addition operator for BasePlotting objects. It concatenates the x_data, y_data, and weights from other into self.
__radd__(self, other): Implements reverse addition. If other is not a BasePlotting instance, it creates a new BasePlotting object and adds self to it.
cdef list __ticks__(self, float s, float e, float st): Internal helper to generate a list of tick values given a start, end, and step.

SaveFigure(self): Generates and saves the plot to a file.
It first calls __resetplt__.
If self.Style is "ATLAS", it applies mplhep.style.ATLAS and sets DPI to 800.
It configures matplotlib.rcParams with various font, axis, legend, and title sizes, and LaTeX settings if UseLateX is enabled.
Calls __compile__ to prepare the plot data.
Sets plot titles, labels, and scales (logarithmic if specified).
Configures x and y-axis ticks based on xStep, yStep, or variable_x_bins/variable_y_bins.
Sets the figure size.
Saves the figure in both its final format and a raw .pgf format (if LaTeX is used).
Includes error handling for plotting failures.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties control the visual appearance and configuration of the plots.

listitem.para: @property Hatch -> str: Gets and sets the hatch pattern for filled areas.
@property DPI -> int: Gets and sets the dots per inch (resolution) for saved figures.
@property Style -> str: Gets and sets the plotting style (e.g., "ATLAS").
@property CapSize -> float: Gets and sets the size of the error bar caps.
@property ErrorBars -> bool: Gets and sets whether error bars should be displayed.
@property Filename -> str: Gets and sets the base filename for saving plots.
@property OutputDirectory -> str: Gets and sets the output directory path for saved plots.
@property FontSize -> float: Gets and sets the base font size for text elements.
@property AxisSize -> float: Gets and sets the font size for axis labels.
@property LegendSize -> float: Gets and sets the font size for the legend.
@property TitleSize -> float: Gets and sets the font size for the plot title.
@property UseLateX -> bool: Gets and sets whether LaTeX rendering should be used for text.
@property xScaling -> float: Gets and sets the scaling factor for the figure's width.
@property yScaling -> float: Gets and sets the scaling factor for the figure's height.
@property AutoScaling -> bool: Gets and sets whether axes should auto-scale.
@property LineStyle -> str: Gets and sets the line style for plots.
@property Title -> str: Gets and sets the main title of the plot. If ShowCount is true and data exists, it appends the sum of counts to the title.
@property xTitle -> str: Gets and sets the label for the x-axis.
@property yTitle -> str: Gets and sets the label for the y-axis. Supports <units> placeholder for bin width.
@property xLogarithmic -> bool: Gets and sets whether the x-axis should be logarithmic.
@property yLogarithmic -> bool: Gets and sets whether the y-axis should be logarithmic.
@property xStep -> float: Gets and sets the step size for x-axis ticks.
@property yStep -> float: Gets and sets the step size for y-axis ticks.
@property xMin -> float: Gets and sets the minimum value for the x-axis. Setting this also sets self.set_xmin = True.
@property yMin -> float: Gets and sets the minimum value for the y-axis. Setting this also sets self.set_ymin = True.
@property xMax -> float: Gets and sets the maximum value for the x-axis. Setting this also sets self.set_xmax = True.
@property yMax -> float: Gets and sets the maximum value for the y-axis. Setting this also sets self.set_ymax = True.
@property Overflow -> str: Gets and sets how histogram overflow bins are handled ("sum" or "none").
@property Color -> str: Gets and sets the primary color for the plot. If not set, a random hex color is generated.
@property Colors -> list: Gets and sets a list of colors to be used for multiple plots.

sect1.title: The TH1F Class
sect1.para: TH1F is a cdef class that inherits from BasePlotting and is designed to represent and plot 1D histograms.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Sets the C++ plotting object's prefix to "TH1F".

__init__(self, inpt = None, **kwargs): Initializes self.Histograms and self.Histogram. If kwargs are provided, they are converted to an inpt dictionary. It then attempts to set various attributes from inpt["data"].
@property xData -> list: Gets and sets the raw x-data for the histogram.
@property xBins: Gets and sets the number of bins for the x-axis (if regular binning) or a list of bin edges (if variable binning).
@property CrossSection -> float: Gets and sets the cross-section value, used for scaling.
@property IntegratedLuminosity -> float: Gets and sets the integrated luminosity, used for scaling.
@property HistFill -> str: Gets and sets the histogram fill style (e.g., "step", "fill").
@property Stacked -> bool: Gets and sets whether multiple histograms should be stacked.
@property LineWidth -> float: Gets and sets the line width for histogram outlines.
@property Alpha -> float: Gets and sets the transparency (alpha) value for filled histograms.
@property Density -> bool: Gets and sets whether the histogram should be normalized to represent a probability density.
@property counts -> list: Returns the counts (values) of the histogram after compilation.
@property Marker -> str: Gets and sets the marker style for error bars or points.
@property xLabels -> dict: Gets and sets a dictionary for custom x-axis labels (for categorical histograms).
@property Weights -> list: Gets and sets a list of weights to be applied to the x-data during histogram filling.
@property ShowCount -> bool: Gets and sets whether the total count should be displayed in the title.
FX(self, val = None): Sets the function to be used for ratio or chi-squared plots. Accepts "ratio", "chi2" (case-insensitive strings), or a callable function. Defaults to ratio.

KStest(self, TH1F hist): Performs a Kolmogorov-Smirnov (KS) test between the current histogram's data and another TH1F histogram.
hist
Another TH1F object for comparison.
The result of scipy.stats.ks_2samp.
cdef __error__(self, vector[float] xarr, vector[float] up, vector[float] low, str label = "Uncertainty", str color = "k"): Internal method to plot error bands using matplotlib.pyplot.fill_between.
cdef __get_error_seg__(self, plot, str label = "Uncertainty", str color = "k"): Extracts error segment data from a matplotlib errorbar plot and calls __error__ to draw the error band.
cdef float scale_f(self): Calculates the scaling factor based on CrossSection and IntegratedLuminosity.
cdef dict factory(self): Creates a dictionary of common plotting parameters for mplhep.histplot.
cdef __build__(self): Constructs a boost_histogram object based on the configured xData, xBins, xMin, xMax, xLabels, Weights, Density, and ApplyScaling.

cdef dict __compile__(self, bool raw = False): The core method for compiling and rendering 1D histograms.
Determines x and y-axis limits.
Handles single histograms, multiple histograms, and stacked histograms.
Integrates ratio/chi-squared plots if Histogram and Histograms are both present.
Manages error bar plotting and legend generation.

sect1.title: The TH2F Class
sect1.para: TH2F is a cdef class that inherits from BasePlotting and is designed to represent and plot 2D histograms.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Sets the C++ plotting object's prefix to "TH2F".

__init__(self, inpt = None, **kwargs): Initializes self.Color to "plasma". If kwargs are provided, they are converted to an inpt dictionary. It then attempts to set various attributes from inpt["data"].
@property yBins: Gets and sets the number of bins for the y-axis (if regular binning) or a list of bin edges (if variable binning).
@property xBins: Gets and sets the number of bins for the x-axis (if regular binning) or a list of bin edges (if variable binning).
@property xLabels -> dict: Gets and sets a dictionary for custom x-axis labels.
@property yLabels -> dict: Gets and sets a dictionary for custom y-axis labels.
@property xData -> list: Gets and sets the raw x-data for the 2D histogram.
@property yData -> list: Gets and sets the raw y-data for the 2D histogram.
@property Weights -> list: Gets and sets a list of weights to be applied to the data during histogram filling.
cdef __build__(self): Constructs a 2D boost_histogram object based on the configured xData, yData, xBins, yBins, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, and Weights.
cdef dict __compile__(self, bool raw = False): The core method for compiling and rendering 2D histograms using mplhep.hist2dplot. It also configures the color bar and axis limits.

sect1.title: The TLine Class
sect1.para: TLine is a cdef class that inherits from BasePlotting and is designed to represent and plot lines or scatter plots with optional error bars.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Sets the C++ plotting object's prefix to "TLine".

__init__(self, inpt = None, **kwargs): Initializes self.Lines and self.Marker. If kwargs are provided, they are converted to an inpt dictionary. It then attempts to set various attributes from inpt["data"].
@property xData -> list: Gets and sets the x-coordinates of the line/points.
@property yData -> list: Gets and sets the y-coordinates of the line/points.
@property Marker -> str: Gets and sets the marker style for points.
@property yDataUp -> list: Gets and sets the upper error values for the y-coordinates.
@property yDataDown -> list: Gets and sets the lower error values for the y-coordinates.
@property LineWidth -> float: Gets and sets the width of the line.

cdef void factory(self): Configures and plots a single line or scatter plot using matplotlib.pyplot.plot or matplotlib.pyplot.errorbar, based on xData, yData, yDataUp, yDataDown, ErrorBars, LineStyle, Color, Marker, LineWidth, CapSize, and Title.

cdef dict __compile__(self, bool raw = False): The core method for compiling and rendering line plots.
Handles plotting of multiple TLine objects stored in self.Lines.
Determines and sets x and y-axis limits.
Configures legend and title.

sect1.title: The ROC Class
sect1.para: ROC is a cdef class that inherits from TLine and is specifically designed for plotting Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curves. It integrates with sklearn.metrics for ROC curve calculation and AUC (Area Under the Curve) computation.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): Initializes self.num_cls, self.inits, self.Binary. Sets the C++ plotting object's prefix to "ROC Curve". Attempts to import sklearn.metrics and sets self.inits accordingly, issuing a warning if import fails.
__init__(self, inpt = None, **kwargs): Initializes self.Lines and self.auc. Sets default xTitle, yTitle, xMax, yMax, xMin, yMin appropriate for ROC curves.

@property Scores: Sets the prediction scores for ROC curve calculation. Accepts various input formats (list of lists, dictionary mapping names/integers to lists/tuples). It calls self.ptr.build_ROC to pass data to the C++ backend.

@property Truth: Sets the true labels for ROC curve calculation. Accepts various input formats (list of integers, dictionary mapping names/integers to lists/tuples). It calls self.ptr.build_ROC to pass data to the C++ backend.
@property Titles -> list: Gets and sets a list of titles for individual ROC curves.
@property AUC -> dict: Returns a dictionary containing the calculated AUC values for each ROC curve.
@property xData (Overridden): Overrides the xData property from TLine. Issues a warning if used, suggesting Scores instead.
@property yData (Overridden): Overrides the yData property from TLine. Issues a warning if used, suggesting Truth instead.
cdef void factory(self): Overrides the factory method from TLine. It is intentionally left empty as ROC curve plotting is handled by __compile__.

cdef dict __compile__(self, bool raw = False): The core method for compiling and rendering ROC curves.
Checks if sklearn was successfully imported and if roc_data is available from the C++ backend.
Uses sklearn.metrics.roc_curve and sklearn.metrics.auc to calculate FPR, TPR, and AUC for each class.
(Commented out in source) Intended to plot individual ROC curves using TLine objects and display AUC values.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The plotting.pyx module extensively uses Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class ...: Declares classes as Cython extension types.
cdef plotting *ptr: A C++ pointer to a plotting object, managed by the Cython class.
from libcpp.string cimport string, from libcpp.vector cimport vector, from libcpp.map cimport map, from libcpp cimport bool, float: Imports C++ standard library types.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from tools.pxd, including env, enc, env_vec, enc_list, as_basic_udict, as_umap, merge_data.
from AnalysisG.core.plotting cimport plotting: Imports the C++ plotting class declaration from its .pxd file.
cython.operator.dereference as dref: Used to dereference C++ pointers.
scipy.stats, matplotlib.pyplot, boost_histogram, mplhep, numpy, random, pathlib, pickle: Python libraries used for statistical tests, plotting, histogramming, numerical operations, random number generation, path manipulation, and object serialization.
